features.description.message: All features of the doQumentation platform — live code execution, Code Engine, simulator mode, multi-language support, learning progress, and more.

features.intro.message: doQumentation turns IBM Quantum's open-source content into an interactive learning platform with live code execution, IBM Cloud Code Engine, simulator mode, learning progress tracking, and support for 20 languages.

features.contentLibrary.pages.desc.message: 42 Tutorials, 171 Guides, 154 Course pages, and 14 Modules — all sourced from IBM Quantum's open-source Qiskit documentation.

features.contentLibrary.sync.desc.message: Content is automatically synced from IBM's GitHub repository, keeping tutorials and courses up to date with the latest Qiskit releases.

features.contentLibrary.nav.desc.message: Auto-generated sidebars with collapsible categories. Tutorials, Guides, Courses, and Modules each have their own organized sidebar.

features.execution.toggle.desc.message: Every notebook page has a Run button that activates all code cells with a live Jupyter kernel. Click Back to return to the static view.

features.execution.backends.desc.message: Free remote execution via Binder or Google Colab, fast serverless kernels via IBM Cloud Code Engine, or full offline via Docker and RasQberry Pi. When multiple backends are available, choose which one to use from the Settings page.

features.execution.ce.desc.message: Serverless Jupyter kernel on your IBM Cloud account. Cold start in seconds instead of Binder's 10–25 minutes. Free tier covers roughly 14 hours per month.

features.execution.workshop.desc.message: Run classroom workshops with up to 50 participants. Deploy multiple Code Engine instances and share a single URL — participants are automatically distributed with sticky sessions and automatic failover.

features.execution.monitoring.desc.message: Live dashboard with auto-refresh (30s) shows per-instance kernel counts, busy kernels, connections, memory usage, and uptime. Each instance exposes a /stats endpoint. Stress test script simulates concurrent users to find capacity limits before the class starts.

features.execution.colab.desc.message: Every notebook page has an 'Open in Colab' button for zero-install cloud execution. Opens translated notebooks when browsing in another language.

features.execution.feedback.desc.message: Visual feedback on every cell: amber border while running, green when done, red on error. A legend appears in the toolbar when the kernel is ready.

features.execution.errors.desc.message: Automatic detection of ModuleNotFoundError, NameError, and kernel disconnection. Actionable hints appear below the cell with suggested fixes. Every error includes a "Report this error" link that opens a pre-filled GitHub issue.

features.execution.pip.desc.message: When a missing package is detected, a clickable 'Install' button appears. After install completes, the failed cell re-runs automatically.

features.execution.lab.desc.message: Open any notebook in full JupyterLab. Binder launches show real-time phase tracking with elapsed timer and cache-miss warnings. Cancel anytime or get slow-phase alerts suggesting faster backends. Subsequent clicks reuse the same server instantly.

features.execution.runall.desc.message: Run All executes every cell in order with a live progress counter. Stop after the current cell anytime. Restart gives you a fresh kernel without losing your Binder session.

features.ibm.credentials.desc.message: Save your IBM Quantum API token and CRN once in Settings. Stored locally in your browser with 7-day auto-expiry for security.

features.ibm.injection.desc.message: Credentials are silently injected into the kernel on startup. No need to paste tokens into every notebook — just click Run.

features.ibm.simulator.desc.message: Run all notebooks without an IBM Quantum account. Choose AerSimulator for ideal simulation, or pick from dozens of FakeBackends that model real device noise. Zero setup required.

features.ibm.badge.desc.message: The toolbar shows which mode is active — the simulator name (e.g. 'FakeSherbrooke') or 'IBM Quantum' — so you always know how your code is running.

features.progress.tracking.desc.message: Pages you visit get a checkmark (✓) in the sidebar. Notebooks you execute get a play indicator (▶). Track your journey through the content.

features.progress.badges.desc.message: Each sidebar category shows a badge like '3/10' so you can see progress at a glance. Click to clear per-section progress.

features.progress.resume.desc.message: The homepage shows a 'Continue where you left off' card with your last visited page and when you were there.

features.progress.bookmarks.desc.message: Star any page to bookmark it. Bookmarks appear on the homepage. Stored locally, up to 50 pages.

features.progress.recent.desc.message: The homepage shows your 5 most recently visited pages for quick access.

features.progress.display.desc.message: Adjust code font size (10–22px), toggle static cell outputs, and suppress Python warnings for cleaner output.

features.progress.onboarding.desc.message: First-time visitors see contextual tips — like "Click Run to execute code live" on notebook pages. Tips auto-dismiss after a few visits.

features.i18n.locales.desc.message: The full site is available in 20 languages including German, Japanese, Spanish, French, and more. Each locale runs on its own subdomain with translated UI.

features.i18n.fallback.desc.message: Untranslated pages fall back to English with a banner in the reader's language, so no page is ever missing.

features.ui.search.desc.message: Full-text search across all 381 pages, works offline. Results appear instantly as you type.

features.ui.dark.desc.message: Full dark theme support. Circuit diagrams and Matplotlib outputs auto-invert for readability.

features.ui.video.desc.message: Course videos with YouTube mapping for reliable playback. Falls back to IBM Video when YouTube is unavailable.

features.ui.docker.desc.message: Multi-stage Docker build with CI/CD to GitHub Container Registry. Full stack (site + Jupyter) or lightweight static-only image.

features.ui.mobile.desc.message: Hamburger navigation, horizontal code scrolling, and responsive card grids. Works on phones and tablets.

features.ui.math.desc.message: KaTeX for fast, high-quality LaTeX math rendering across all content pages — equations, matrices, and quantum notation.

settings.advanced.saveSuccess.message: Settings saved! Refresh the page to apply.

settings.advanced.clearSuccess.message: Custom settings cleared. Using auto-detected configuration.

settings.ibm.invalidCrn.message: CRN must start with "crn:" (e.g. crn:v1:bluemix:...). Leave blank if unsure.

settings.ibm.saveSuccess.message: Credentials saved! They will be auto-injected when the kernel starts.

settings.ce.saveSuccess.message: IBM Cloud Code Engine settings saved! Code will now execute via your instance.

settings.ce.deleteSuccess.message: IBM Cloud Code Engine settings cleared. Falling back to Binder.

settings.description.message: Configure Jupyter server for code execution

settings.intro.message: Configure the Jupyter server used for executing Python code in tutorials.

settings.backend.description.message: Choose which backend to use for code execution:

settings.ce.daysRemaining.message: IBM Cloud Code Engine settings will auto-delete in {days} day(s).

settings.info.ceUrl.message: Your Code Engine app URL, OR comma-separated URLs / base64 pool config from a workshop instructor.

settings.ce.urlHint.message: For workshops: paste comma-separated URLs or the base64 pool config from your instructor.

settings.info.ceToken.message: The JUPYTER_TOKEN value you set when creating the IBM Cloud Code Engine app.

settings.info.executionMode.message: Choose how quantum circuits are executed when you click Run on tutorial pages.

settings.executionMode.desc.message: Choose what happens when you click Run on tutorial pages. This applies to embedded code execution on this site only — opening a notebook in JupyterLab uses the standard Qiskit runtime.

settings.executionMode.noneDesc.message: Manage credentials and backend in code cells yourself.

settings.executionMode.aerDesc.message: Ideal simulation, no noise. Fast, works for all circuits.

settings.executionMode.fakeDesc.message: Simulates real IBM device noise. More realistic but slower.

settings.executionMode.deviceHint.message: Device list is updated automatically when you run code.

settings.executionMode.ibmDesc.message: Connect to real quantum hardware via IBM Quantum credentials.

settings.executionMode.noCredentials.message: IBM Quantum mode selected but no credentials saved. Enter your token and CRN below.

settings.executionMode.applyHint.message: Changes take effect on the next kernel session. If code is running, click Back then Run to apply.

settings.ibm.securityNote.message: {strong} Credentials are stored in your browser's localStorage in plain text. They are not encrypted and can be read by browser extensions or anyone with access to this device. Use the expiry setting below to limit exposure, and delete credentials when you're done. For shared or public computers, prefer the manual {saveAccount} method described below instead.

settings.ibm.autoInjectDesc.message: Enter your IBM Quantum credentials once here. They will be auto-injected via {saveAccount} when the kernel starts, so you don't need to enter them in every notebook. This applies to embedded code execution on this site only — opening a notebook in JupyterLab requires calling {saveAccount} manually.

settings.ibm.step2.message: {strong} — go to {link}, click "Create instance +", select the Open (free) plan, and follow the wizard

settings.ibm.step3.message: {strong} — back on the {link}, find your instance under "Instances" and click the copy icon next to "CRN"

settings.ibm.step4.message: {strong} — on the {link}, find "API key" and click "Create +"

settings.ibm.guideLink.message: Need more help? See {link} for screenshots and detailed steps.

settings.info.apiToken.message: Find your API token at quantum.cloud.ibm.com under your profile.

settings.info.crn.message: Cloud Resource Name — copy from the Instances page on IBM Quantum Platform.

settings.info.ibmPlan.message: Open Plan does not support Sessions. When set to Open, Session calls are automatically converted to job mode so tutorials run without errors.

settings.ibm.manualSummary.message: Alternative: Run save_account() manually in a notebook cell

settings.ibm.manualDesc.message: If you prefer not to store credentials in this browser, paste this into any code cell and run it. Credentials are saved in the Binder kernel's temporary storage and lost when the session ends.

settings.display.outputs.desc.message: Each notebook page shows pre-computed outputs (images, tables, text) from IBM's original runs. When you click {run} to execute code live, both the original outputs and your new live results are shown side by side. Enable this toggle to hide the original outputs during live execution, keeping only your results visible.

settings.display.outputs.hidden.message: Pre-computed outputs hidden during live execution

settings.display.outputs.shown.message: Show both pre-computed and live outputs

settings.display.warnings.desc.message: By default, Python warnings (deprecation notices, runtime hints) are suppressed for cleaner notebook output. Disable this to see all warnings — useful for debugging or learning about API changes.

settings.data.privacy.message: All data is stored locally in your browser (localStorage). Nothing is sent to our servers. Clearing your browser data or using a different browser/device starts fresh.

settings.progress.desc.message: Visited pages show a {check} in the sidebar; executed notebooks show a {play}.

settings.progress.empty.message: No progress tracked yet. Visit tutorials and guides to start tracking.

settings.bookmarks.count.message: {count} bookmarked page(s) — shown on the homepage

settings.bookmarks.empty.message: No bookmarks yet. Use the bookmark button on any page to save it here.

settings.resetAll.description.message: Remove all saved data including progress, bookmarks, display preferences, and credentials.

settings.resetAll.confirm.message: This will remove all your progress, bookmarks, preferences, and saved credentials. This cannot be undone. Continue?

settings.ce.description.message: IBM Cloud Code Engine provides a fast, serverless Jupyter kernel powered by your own IBM Cloud account. Startup takes seconds instead of minutes. Free tier covers ~14 hours/month.

settings.ce.step1.message: Create an IBM Cloud account at {link} (free tier available)

settings.ce.step2.message: Go to the {link} and create a new project in your preferred region

settings.ce.step3.message: Create a new application with image {image}, listening port 8080

settings.ce.step3sizing.message: Sizing: 1 vCPU / 2 GB for single user, 8 vCPU / 16 GB for workshops (up to 80 users)

settings.ce.step4.message: Set environment variables: {token} to a secure token (min 32 characters) and {cors} to your domain (e.g. {domain})

settings.ce.sizingNote.message: For workshop sizing details, see the workshop setup documentation.

settings.binder.desc.message: When running on GitHub Pages, code executes via {mybinder}. The Binder environment includes core Qiskit packages pre-installed:

settings.binder.installHint.message: Some notebooks require additional packages. You can install them on demand by running this in a code cell:

settings.binder.installAll.message: Or install all optional packages at once:

settings.help.rasqberry.desc.message: If you're running on a RasQberry Pi, the Jupyter server should be automatically detected. If not, ensure the jupyter-tutorials service is running:

settings.help.local.desc.message: Start a Jupyter server with CORS enabled:

settings.help.docker.desc.message: The Docker container generates a random Jupyter token at startup. Code execution through the website (port 8080) works automatically — no token needed. The token is only required for direct JupyterLab access on port 8888.

settings.help.docker.retrieveToken.message: To retrieve the token from container logs:

settings.help.remote.desc.message: For remote servers, ensure CORS is configured to allow connections from this site. Add the following to your {config}:

qamposer.description.message: Experimental visual quantum circuit composer — build circuits with drag-and-drop gates and run them on your configured backend.

qamposer.experimental.message: Experimental — interface may change. Not yet linked from the main navigation.

qamposer.intro.message: Build quantum circuits visually with drag-and-drop gates from QAMPoser. Circuits are simulated on the same Jupyter kernel used by doQumentation's interactive tutorials, so they honor your Simulator Mode and IBM Quantum settings automatically.

qamposer.howItWorks.editor.message: Drag gates from the operations panel onto the circuit. The OpenQASM code updates in real time.

qamposer.howItWorks.simulate.message: Clicking Run Simulation sends the circuit to the active Jupyter kernel — the same kernel used by tutorial pages.

qamposer.howItWorks.routing.message: The execution target is determined by your doQumentation settings: ideal simulator, noisy fake backend, or real IBM Quantum hardware.

qamposer.howItWorks.realDevice.message: Real-hardware runs require an explicit confirmation each time, and the live preview is automatically disabled in real-device mode to avoid accidental job submissions.

betaNotice.text.message: This project is in beta. Found a bug or have an idea? {issueLink} — we'd love your feedback!

executable.errorHint.notDefined.message: is not defined. Run the cells above first — notebooks must be executed in order.

executable.error.sessionNotSupported.message: Sessions are not available on the IBM Quantum Open Plan.

executable.error.sessionSettingsLink.message: Set your plan type to "Open" in Settings

executable.error.sessionSettingsAfter.message: to automatically convert Session calls to job mode.

executable.skipCell.description: Bold label on save_account() cells when simulator/credentials active

executable.skipCell.simulatorActive.message: Simulator Mode is active. Running it has no effect.
executable.skipCell.simulatorActive.description: Explanation shown on save_account() cells when simulator mode is on

executable.skipCell.credentialsBefore.message: your credentials are already configured via
executable.skipCell.credentialsBefore.description: Text before the Settings link on save_account() cells (include trailing space if needed)

executable.skipCell.settingsLink.description: Link text pointing to the Settings page

executable.skipCell.credentialsAfter.message: . Running it with placeholder values will overwrite them.
executable.skipCell.credentialsAfter.description: Text after the Settings link on save_account() cells (include leading punctuation)

executable.info.skipCell.message: Your credentials are already injected automatically. Running this cell would overwrite them.

executable.openPlan.sessionPatched.message: Open Plan: Session automatically replaced with job mode

executable.placeholder.willInject.message: Your credentials from Settings will be used automatically. You can skip this cell.

executable.placeholder.configure.message: Configure your IBM Quantum credentials in

executable.reset.confirm.message: This will clear all execution results and return to static view. Continue?

executable.restart.confirm.message: Restart kernel? All variables and outputs will be cleared.

executable.clearSession.confirm.message: Clear session? You will need to wait for a new server on next Run.

executable.status.error.message: Disconnected — click Back, then Run to retry

executable.button.cancelTitle.message: Cancel Binder startup and return to static view

executable.button.runBinderTitle.message: Execute via Binder (may take a moment to start)

executable.button.restartTitle.message: Restart kernel (clears all variables and outputs)

executable.info.restartKernel.message: Clear all variables and outputs, start fresh. Same server session.

executable.button.runAllTitle.message: Run all cells on this page in order

executable.button.clearSessionTitle.message: Clear session and return to static view (next Run starts a new server)

executable.info.clearSession.message: End this Jupyter server session. Next Run will start a fresh server.

executable.info.ceStatus.message: Code Engine is starting a cloud container with all packages. This usually takes 1–3 minutes.

executable.info.binderStatus.message: Binder is preparing a free cloud server with all packages. This may take 2–25 minutes depending on cache availability.

executable.info.legend.message: Colored bars on the left edge of each code cell show execution status.

executable.badge.settingsTitle.message: Go to Settings to change execution mode

executable.info.modeBadge.message: Click to change execution mode in Settings

executable.status.ceCacheMiss.message: ⚠ Cold start — container build may take a few minutes.

executable.status.binderCacheMiss.message: ⚠ Cache not warmed — total build time 10–25 min. Use Colab (above) or come back later.

executable.info.ceCacheMiss.message: The Code Engine container is being built. This is usually faster than Binder.

executable.info.cacheMiss.message: The Binder Docker image must be rebuilt from scratch. Try Colab for instant access, or come back in ~20 minutes.

executable.status.ceSlowStartup.message: Code Engine startup is taking longer than expected. You can cancel and try again later, or use Colab or Docker instead.

executable.status.binderSlowStartup.message: Binder startup is taking longer than expected. You can cancel and try again later, or use one of the other backends (Colab, Docker, or Code Engine).

executable.binderHint.message: Need extra packages? Run {pipCode} in a cell, or see {packagesLink}.

openInLab.workshopBanner.message: Community workshop notebook — not part of the official IBM Quantum documentation.

openInLab.info.openIn.message: JupyterLab: full notebook editor with all packages pre-installed. Colab: Google's free cloud notebooks (requires a Google account).

tutorialFeedback.hint.message: (website, code execution, navigation — tutorial content is by IBM Quantum)
